summary: Iteration 12 — Home page locked to exactly 4 addon shelves: VERIFIED PASS. All 6 review requirements green. With a non-kids profile seeded into localStorage (id=test-iter12) and Cinemeta resolved (12s wait), the [data-testid=shelves-region] contains EXACTLY 4 [data-testid^=shelf-] elements in this DOM order: shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-movie-top, shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-series-top, shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-movie-year, shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-series-year. Their h2 titles read 'Popular movies', 'Popular series', 'New movies', 'New series' in that exact top-to-bottom order. No other addon shelves (trending / anime / channels / search / popular-other) leak through — every shelf id ends in one of the 4 allowed suffixes. Filter views at /?filter=movie and /?filter=series remain unaffected and still render their full TabGridView grid (100 focusable poster tiles each, well above the home rail's 18-item cap). D-pad navigation works exactly as required: ArrowDown from the first poster of 'Popular movies' walks movie-top → series-top → movie-year → series-year; ArrowUp walks back through series-year → movie-year → series-top → movie-top → network-netflix → hero-info-button. Continue Watching / For You components remain separate from the 4-shelf addon filter — addon-shelf count remained 4 even after seeding their data sources, confirming they are not inside the filtered list. 0 console errors across all flows.

tests_executed.frontend_exactly_4_addon_shelves_on_home: PASS — [data-testid=shelves-region] contains EXACTLY 4 [data-testid^=shelf-] children after 12s Cinemeta resolution. Captured ids: ['shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-movie-top', 'shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-series-top', 'shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-movie-year', 'shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-series-year'].
tests_executed.frontend_shelf_titles_exact_text_and_ord: PASS — Sequential h2 textContent values exactly equal ['Popular movies', 'Popular series', 'New movies', 'New series']. Order preserved as defined in Home.jsx `wanted` array.
tests_executed.frontend_no_unauthorized_addon_shelves: PASS — All 4 shelf ids verified to end with -movie-top / -series-top / -movie-year / -series-year. No trending / anime / channels / search / popular-other shelves leak onto the Home rail.
tests_executed.frontend_filter_movie_renders_full_grid: PASS — Navigated to /?filter=movie, waited 8s. [data-testid=tab-grid-list-movie] present with 100 focusable poster tiles. Far exceeds the home rail's 18-item slice, confirming TabGridView does NOT use the filtered shelves array.
tests_executed.frontend_filter_series_renders_full_grid: PASS — Navigated to /?filter=series, waited 8s. [data-testid=tab-grid-list-series] present with 100 focusable poster tiles.
tests_executed.frontend_dpad_arrow_down_walks_4_shelves: PASS — Focus first poster of shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-movie-top. ArrowDown ×3 visits in exact sequence: movie-top → series-top → movie-year → series-year. Each press lands focus inside a different shelf section.
tests_executed.frontend_dpad_arrow_up_walks_back_throug: PASS — From series-year first poster, ArrowUp ×6 visits in order: series-year → movie-year → series-top → movie-top → network-netflix (networks shelf) → hero-info-button (hero billboard). At no point does focus skip a row.
tests_executed.frontend_continue_watching_and_for_you_n: PASS — Addon-shelf count stayed at exactly 4 both before and after seeding vesper:continue-watching and vesper:viewing-style localStorage for the active profile. ContinueWatchingShelf and ForYouShelf are independent components and are NOT counted in [data-testid^=shelf-].

critical_code_review_comments: Home.jsx:65-94 — `shelves` useMemo iterates `wanted` (length 4) for every render and does Array.prototype.find against `all` for each → O(4·N) on every liveShelves change. Negligible at N≈4-10 but worth noting if catalog count balloons. Could collapse to a single pass over `all` keyed by suffix.

Home.jsx:319-323 — Lazy wrapper with `eager={i < 3}` means the 4th shelf ('New series') is lazy-loaded. Given the page now has only 4 shelves and the user explicitly wants all 4 visible quickly on the HK1 box, consider `eager={true}` for all 4 (the whole point of trimming was to make the page render fast — lazy-deferring the last of 4 saves almost nothing).

Home.jsx:84 — `s.id && s.id.endsWith(w.suffix)` couples this filter to Cinemeta's id convention. If any future addon ships top/year catalogs with a different id pattern (e.g. `com.foo.bar:popular_movies`), they'll be silently dropped from the Home rail. A more robust match would be on `cat.id === 'top' && cat.type === 'movie'` etc., but that would require carrying type/catId on the shelf shape (currently only `id` and `title` are exposed). Flagging as a future-proofing note; current Cinemeta-only behaviour is what the user asked for.

Home.jsx:34 — `const shelfFilter = isFilterView ? null : null;` — both branches return null. Looks like a leftover from when filter views consumed live shelves. Can be simplified to `const shelfFilter = null;` or removed entirely.
ProfileEdit.jsx still 1960 lines (carried from iter 7-11). Long-standing review comment, still outstanding.
test_credentials: None required — non-kids profile seeded via Playwright addInitScript into localStorage (id=test-iter12, name=TestIter12, avatarId=a1, kids=false). No backend auth involved.
seed_data_creation: Playwright addInitScript wrote one transient profile to onnowtv-profiles-v1 + set onnowtv-active-profile-v1='test-iter12' before navigating to /. For the CW/FY independence check, also injected vesper:continue-watching:test-iter12 (1 entry) and vesper:viewing-style:test-iter12 (genres + items) via page.evaluate then reload. No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 12 shipped: Home.jsx shelves useMemo now filters liveShelves to exactly 4 by id suffix [-movie-top, -series-top, -movie-year, -series-year], overrides each title to 'Popular movies' / 'Popular series' / 'New movies' / 'New series', and emits them in that exact DOM order. Cinemeta's resolved addon id is `com.linvo.cinemeta` so the 4 testids are shelf-com.linvo.cinemeta-{movie-top,series-top,movie-year,series-year}. Filter tab views (/?filter=movie, /?filter=series) are UNCHANGED — they use TabGridView with its own useTabCatalog hook and render ~100 items each. ContinueWatchingShelf + ForYouShelf + NetworksShelf are separate components rendered above the shelves loop in Home.jsx and are NOT inside `[data-testid^=shelf-]`. Useful selectors: [data-testid=shelves-region], [data-testid^=shelf-], [data-testid=tab-grid-list-movie], [data-testid=tab-grid-list-series], [data-testid=networks-shelf], [data-testid=hero-billboard], [data-testid=hero-info-button], [data-focusable=true]. Seed profile via addInitScript writing onnowtv-profiles-v1 + onnowtv-active-profile-v1 BEFORE page load; otherwise the app may redirect to /profiles for first-time users. Wait 10-12s after navigation for live shelves to resolve.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. All 6 review requirements verified PASS on the live preview URL.
